title: syzygium rugosum var. cordatum
intro: Syzygium rugosum var. cordatum, also known as the water apple or Java apple, is a type of fruit-bearing plant native to Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Syzygium rugosum var. cordatum make it beneficial for various health conditions, including fever reduction, digestive issues, and skin problems, as well as providing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant can be used to prepare various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be consumed fresh or dried to reap its health benefits.
